title: fix(gen-text): strip stream_options for non-OpenAI Azure models
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Azure Foundry rejects `stream_options` for non-OpenAI deployments (Mistral, Grok, DeepSeek, Llama) with `422 extra_forbidden`; the gateway injects it on every stream.
- Strips `stream_options` when provider is `azure-openai` and the deployment isn't an OpenAI model, reusing the existing `isOpenAIModel` gate. OpenAI Azure models keep it.
- Strips top-level `null`/`undefined` provider options at the upstream boundary without recursing into nested messages, tool schemas, or JSON schemas.
- Adds focused tests for Azure strip/keep paths and provider payload cleanup — 17/17 local tests pass.

Note: the exact model in the issue title (`mistral-small-2503`) has since moved to OpenRouter, but the bug class is still live for current `azure-openai` non-OpenAI deployments (grok-4-20, Mistral-Large-3, Llama-4-Maverick, etc.).

Risks: low — gated on `azure-openai` + non-OpenAI deployment id; OpenAI Azure streaming usage reporting is unaffected. The null cleanup only affects top-level provider options that should mean “unset”.

Fixes #10902

gist.headline: Azure streams unblocked
gist.blurb: Fixed a pesky bug where Azure would reject streaming requests for non-OpenAI models like Mistral and Grok. The spice must flow, and so must the tokens.
gist.summary: The text generation gateway now strips the stream_options parameter for non-OpenAI models on Azure (like Mistral, Grok, and Llama) to prevent 422 extra_forbidden errors.
gist.impact: Developers can now successfully stream responses from non-OpenAI models hosted on Azure Foundry without encountering validation errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a plumber, unclogging a glowing blue (Azure) pipe so that data can flow smoothly. Nomnom creature happily eating the rejected 'stream_options' tags.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny plumber's hat, unclogging a glowing blue pipe while the round Nomnom creature happily eats discarded 'stream_options' tags. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel blue gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
